key_insight: PBE-trained machine-learning interatomic potentials can wrongly predict HfO2’s ground state by overstabilizing a low-density I41/amd phase over the experimentally observed P21/c structure.
engineering_relevance: For computational materials workflows, this means phase stability and ferroelectric switching predictions can be unreliable if the underlying DFT reference functional is biased, especially for structures with large lattice relaxations.
method: Compare PBE-based MLIP predictions against known HfO2 phase ordering
Contrast energies from multiple DFT functionals, including PBE, PBEsol, and LDA
Check impact on polarization-switching energy landscapes and barrier heights
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that both a custom PBE-based MLIP and widely used foundation models (NequIP-OAM-L, MatterSim-v1-5M) predict I41/amd as lower in energy than P21/c, matching the same error seen with PBE DFT data.
watch_item: Do not assume a PBE-trained MLIP is reliable for crystal-phase ranking in HfO2; functional choice can systematically bias low-density octahedral phases and alter switching barriers.
paper_title: A wrong ground-state structure of HfO$_2$ predicted by machine-learning interatomic potentials based on the PBE functional
